content.problemStatement:
There is a binary string SSS. Alice and Bob are playing a game on it.

Each turn, the following happens:


Alice selects a 010101 subsequence in SSS and removes it from SSS. (a 010101 subsequence is a length 222 subsequence whose first character is 000 and second character is 111)
Bob selects a 010101 subsequence in SSS and removes it from SSS.
After both players have moved, the turn is called completed and Alice scores 111 point. Then, the next turn begins.
If either player is unable to move (there isn't any 010101 subsequence left in SSS), that turn ends immediately and the game ends.

Alice tries to maximize her points, while Bob tries to minimize Alice's points. Find how many points Alice would score if both players play optimally.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines contains a binary string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer — Alice's score when both players play optimally.
constraints: The sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ across all tests doesn't exceed  2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
The sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ across all tests doesn't exceed 100100100
The sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ across all tests doesn't exceed 150015001500

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first example, this is one of the possible outcomes.
Turn 111:

Alice removes subsequence (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) from SSS, so SSS becomes 010101.
Bob removes subsequence (3,6)(3, 6)(3,6) from SSS, so SSS becomes 0110.
Alice scores one point and the first turn ends.

Turn 2:

Alice removes subsequence (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) from SSS, so SSS becomes 10.
Bob has nothing to do, so the game ends immediately.

Thus, Alice has one point in total.
